---
title: "R Notebook"
output: html_notebook
---

Visualization

```{r}
library(ggplot2)
library(sp)
library(tidyverse)
library(sf)
library(dplyr)
library(lwgeom)
```

## Load the data 
```{r}
data <- st_read('../../APP/dados_tratados/all_app_tratado.shp')
```

## Visualize 

### Histograms

-- Tamanho ded propriedade(ha)
```{r}
library(scales) # For pretty axis formatting

# Histogram 1: Total Area (with log transformation)
p1 <- ggplot(data, aes(x = area_tot)) +
  geom_histogram(fill = "#1f77b4", color = "white", alpha = 0.8, 
                 bins = 30, boundary = 0) +
  scale_x_log10(
   # limits = c(0,100000),
    labels = trans_format("log10", math_format(10^.x)),
    breaks = trans_breaks("log10", function(x) 10^x)
  ) +
  labs(title = "Distribution of Total Area (Log Scale)",
       x = "Total Area (hec)",
       y = "Count") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold"),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 12)
  )

print(p1)
```

-- Tamanho de APP 
```{r}
# Histogram 2: App Area
p2 <- ggplot(data, aes(x = area_app)) +
  geom_histogram(fill = "#ff7f0e", color = "white", alpha = 0.8, 
                 bins = 30) +
  scale_x_log10(
   # limits = c(0,100000),
    labels = trans_format("log10", math_format(10^.x)),
    breaks = trans_breaks("log10", function(x) 10^x)
  ) + 
 # scale_x_continuous(labels = comma) +
  labs(title = "Distribution of App Area",
       x = "App Area (m²)",
       y = "Count") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold"),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 12)
  )

print(p2)
```
-- Discrete the area.

 It returns chunks of areas e.g: areas lesser than 10 hec.
 
 Inclui uma nova coluna chamada area_tot_cat contendo os intervalos
 
 
```{r}
library(dplyr)

data <- data %>%
  mutate(area_tot_cat = cut(area_tot,
    breaks = c(0, 10.5, 50.5, 100.5, Inf),
    labels = c("0-10 hec", "11-50 hec", "51-100 hec", ">100 hec"),
    right = FALSE,  # Ensures [lower, upper) interval
    include.lowest = TRUE))

# Verify the transformation
#data %>%
#  count(area_tot_cat) %>%
#  arrange(area_tot_cat)
```
```{r}
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)

# First ensure correct ordering of categories
data <- data %>%
  mutate(area_tot_cat = factor(area_tot_cat,
                               levels = c("0-10 hec", "11-50 hec", "51-100 hec", ">100 hec"),
                               ordered = TRUE))

# Create the plot
ggplot(data, aes(x = area_tot_cat)) +
  geom_bar(fill = "#2c7bb6", alpha = 0.85, width = 0.8) +
  geom_text(aes(label = after_stat(count)), 
            stat = "count", 
            vjust = -0.5, 
            color = "black",
            size = 3.5) +
  labs(title = "Property Size Distribution",
       subtitle = "Categorized total area ranges",
       x = "Total Area Categories",
       y = "Number of Properties",
       caption = "Data source: Your dataset name") +
  scale_x_discrete(drop = FALSE) +  # Show all categories even if empty
  scale_y_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.1))) +  # Add headroom for labels
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 16, hjust = 0.5),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 12, hjust = 0.5, color = "gray50"),
    axis.title.x = element_text(size = 12, margin = margin(t = 10)),
    axis.title.y = element_text(size = 12, margin = margin(r = 10)),
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, vjust = 1),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
    plot.caption = element_text(color = "gray60", margin = margin(t = 10))
  )
```
